title: Electrical Communications (1-A) — ECE 303
excerpt: Foundations of analog and digital electrical communication systems.
content: This course covers the theoretical foundations of analog and digital electrical communication systems, including modulation techniques, signal analysis, and noise performance.

title: Electrical Communications (1-B) — ECE 304
excerpt: Digital communications: modulation, coding, and performance analysis.
content: Advanced digital communications course covering digital modulation schemes, error-control coding, and system performance analysis in AWGN and fading channels.

title: Optical Communications — ECE 502
excerpt: Fiber-optic systems, laser sources, and receivers.
content: This course introduces optical communication systems including fiber-optic technology, laser diode sources, photodetectors, and system design for long-haul and short-reach applications.

title: Digital Signal Processing — ECE 401
excerpt: DFT, FFT algorithms, and digital filter design.
content: Comprehensive coverage of digital signal processing techniques including discrete Fourier transform, fast FFT algorithms, FIR and IIR filter design, and practical applications in communications.

title: Mobile Communication Systems — ECE 503
excerpt: Cellular architectures, 4G/5G, and wireless channel modeling.
content: Study of mobile communication systems from 2G to 5G including cellular architectures, multiple access techniques, OFDM, MIMO systems, and wireless channel modeling.

title: Information Theory & Coding — ECE 504
excerpt: Entropy, channel capacity, and error-correcting codes.
content: Introduction to information theory and coding including source coding, channel coding, Shannon's theorems, convolutional codes, turbo codes, and LDPC codes.
